summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of Star Technologies app. All 15 backend pytest tests passed (15/15). Full E2E frontend flow tested via Playwright: landing render, contact form submission, admin login, dashboard listing, mark-as-replied, logout. All critical flows work end-to-end. RESEND_API_KEY intentionally empty — emails skipped gracefully (admin_email_id/customer_email_id null, email_delivered=false) and contact still returns success:true as designed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'); consider migrating to lifespan context manager (non-blocking, Starlette deprecation).
CORS_ORIGINS defaults to '*' while allow_credentials=True — this combination is rejected by browsers per CORS spec and is unsafe for prod. Should be tightened to explicit origins for production.
ADMIN_PASSWORD is read from .env in plain text and stored as bcrypt hash on seed; idempotent re-hash logic is correct. bcrypt hash format verified to start with $2b$ at runtime.
JWT_SECRET is checked-in to .env file in repo — for production should be rotated and not committed.
Brute-force lockout (5 fails) NOT implemented — login endpoint has no rate-limiting. Mentioned in playbook but not present; acceptable for MVP but flag for hardening.
Frontend uses localStorage for JWT (st_admin_token) instead of httpOnly cookies — vulnerable to XSS. Acceptable per current design but flag for security hardening.
Landing.jsx is large (>700 lines) per file-size guideline; consider splitting into section components.
seed_data_creation: Tests created several TEST_-prefixed contact messages via /api/contact; non-destructive to seeded admin.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers health, contact CRUD, auth, admin endpoints (15 tests, all green). Use ADMIN_EMAIL=Admin@startechnologies.com PASSWORD=Sourav0**. RESEND key intentionally empty — do NOT flag email_delivered=false. Frontend uses Bearer JWT in localStorage 'st_admin_token'. CORS allow_origins='*' with allow_credentials=True is a known config concern. Brute-force lockout NOT implemented. Landing page is large monolithic JSX.
